entities.name: Careerspan
entities.context: V's company; career platform being developed with specific UX, pricing, and positioning strategy

entities.name: Ilya
entities.context: Collaborator who did early pricing work for Careerspan

entities.name: Auto-Apply
entities.context: Feature concept where past effort yields near one-click applications to new opportunities, must be transparent and consent-based

entities.name: Continued Benefit from Past Effort
entities.context: UX principle that candidates should keep gaining value from previously submitted information

entities.name: Better Decisions with Less Cognitive Load
entities.context: Core positioning differentiator for Careerspan vs. competitors that sell scale

entities.name: ATS
entities.context: Traditional Applicant Tracking Systems; Careerspan aims to surpass the experience they offer

entities.name: Page-Level Drop-Off Analytics
entities.context: Analytics approach deemed critical for iterating the application flow

entities.name: Abundant-Dimension Pricing
entities.context: Pricing framework where each package makes one dimension feel abundant (seats, time) while constraining others to preserve margins

entities.name: Transparency and Consent
entities.context: Operational constraint that auto-apply must never submit silent applications; requires explicit user confirmations for edge cases

relationships.context: Did early pricing work suggesting competitive pricing with quality emphasis

relationships.to: Better Decisions with Less Cognitive Load
relationships.context: Core external narrative positioning; sells decision quality, not scale
Planned feature to create continued benefit from past candidate effort
Auto-apply must be consent-based with explicit confirmations for edge cases like relocation and salary
Careerspan aims to surpass the candidate experience traditional ATSs offer
Pricing packages designed to make one dimension abundant while constraining others for margin
Analytics on drop-offs critical for iterating the auto-apply flow
Core UX principle that candidates should keep extracting value from prior submissions
